overview.summary: A skilled threat actor group launches a multi-stage cyber attack against a healthcare organization, utilizing spear phishing, remote file execution, persistence mechanisms, privilege escalation, defense evasion, and ultimately causing data destruction to disrupt critical operations.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disruption of healthcare services and potential data extortion

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers send spear phishing emails containing malicious attachments to healthcare staff. These emails appear legitimate, tricking employees into opening the attachment, which exploits a known vulnerability (CVE-2010-4398) to gain initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Once initial access is established, the attackers exploit a remote file execution vulnerability in outdated medical software. This allows them to execute malicious code remotely, further compromising internal systems.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: To maintain persistence, the attackers establish a reverse shell connection. This provides them with a backdoor to continuously access the compromised system, ensuring they retain control even after reboots or disconnections.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.technique_name: Windows Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
stage.description: The attackers escalate their privileges by modifying the Windows Registry to run malicious scripts at startup. This ensures their malware persists across sessions and gains higher-level access within the infected system.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: To evade detection, the attackers disable or remove antivirus software. This action prevents the security system from identifying and blocking malicious activities, allowing the attackers to operate undetected longer.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers destroy sensitive patient records and critical data, rendering the healthcare facility unable to provide essential services. This disruption undermines trust and poses significant risks to patient care and data integrity.

Scenario.summary: The attack begins with a spear phishing campaign targeting healthcare staff, followed by exploitation of vulnerabilities to gain and maintain access. The attackers escalate privileges, disable security measures, and ultimately destroy critical data, causing widespread disruption to healthcare operations.
